zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Marmion於1313年出生在英國越可爾韋斯特 坦費爾德時，她的父親John Marmion 4th Baron Marmion of Winteringham21歲，母親Maud Furnival34歲。她和John Bernanke至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1361年10月2日在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷享年48歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾塔特沙拉爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Marmion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: perhaps an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name. Compare   Merriman  .
English (Lancashire; of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Old French marmion ‘monkey, brat’.

variants.name: Merriman
meanings: nickname from Middle English mirie, merie, murie ‘merry, pleasant’ + man ‘man’. Compare Merry 1. This surname is established in Ireland (perhaps as an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name; compare Marmion ) as well as England.

there has probably been some confusion with Marriman, probably an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’, or perhaps a pet form of Mary using the hypocoristic suffix -man. The surname Marriman is now vanishingly rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marmon
meanings: variant of Marriman, an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Marmion was born in 1313, in West Tanfield, Yorkshire, England, her father, John Marmion 4th Baron Marmion of Winteringham, was 21 and her mother, Maud Furnival, was 34. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with John Bernanke. She died on 2 October 1361, in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48, and was buried in Tattershall, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Marmion est née en 1313, à West Tanfield, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John Marmion 4th Baron Marmion of Winteringham, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Maud Furnival, avait 34 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec John Bernanke. Elle est décédée le 2 octobre 1361, à Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Tattershall, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Marmionが1313年にイギリスヨークシュアイアウエスト タンフィールドで生まれた時，父John Marmion 4th Baron Marmion of Winteringhamは21歳，母Maud Furnivalは34歳であった。彼女はJohn Bernankeとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1361年10月2日にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアメルトン モウブレイで48歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアタッテルシャルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Marmion(이)가 1313년 영국 요르크실르 웨스트 탠필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Marmion 4th Baron Marmion of Winteringham(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Furnival(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 John Bernanke(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 10월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 멜턴 모우브라이에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 타터샤에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Marmion родилась в 1313 г. в местности вест танфиелд, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Marmion 4th Baron Marmion of Winteringham было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Maud Furnival, 34. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от John Bernanke. Она умерла 2 октября 1361 г. в местности мелтон мавбрэи, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности таттиршолл, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.
